meta_title: For Founders: testing automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Testing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use testing automation to overcome the no-portfolio barrier. A professional case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS founders.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build high-value automated testing suites for your MVP. By demonstrating robust reliability and bug-free releases, you shift the client focus from 'past experience' to 'current engineering excellence.' Use Antigravity for test generation and Claude Code to execute, ensuring your product is production-ready, scalable, and professional.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is simple: your clients do not care about your resume; they care about your uptime. When you lack a portfolio, you are fighting a war of perception. Every bug is a nail in the coffin of your reputation, and every crash is a reason for a potential lead to look elsewhere.

But there is a shortcut. By weaponizing automated testing, you transform your MVP from a 'risky experiment' into an 'enterprise-grade solution.' This approach forces you to build with discipline, resulting in code that is inherently more stable than your competitors. Stop selling your history and start selling your system.

steps.title: Define the Testing Scope
steps.description: Identify the three most critical revenue-generating paths in your app. Testing everything is a waste of time; testing the checkout flow and user onboarding is a business imperative.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my codebase and identify the 3 critical user journeys. Generate a comprehensive test plan for these paths, focusing on edge cases that would cause a business-critical failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a risk assessment report for the current codebase and suggest 3 high-impact E2E test scenarios.'

steps.title: Automate Integration Tests
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate tests that verify your database interactions. This proves your backend is robust enough to handle real-world data without corruption.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create integration tests for the user authentication and payment processing modules using Playwright. Ensure all database mocks are handled correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create integration test suite for /auth and /payments using Playwright.'

steps.title: Implement Visual Regression
steps.description: Visual bugs destroy credibility. Automate UI snapshots to ensure your frontend looks perfect on all devices, proving your attention to detail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup visual regression testing for the main dashboard and landing page. Ensure the tests flag any CSS deviations exceeding 2 pixels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure visual regression testing tools to monitor the dashboard UI.'

steps.title: Execute CI/CD Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Automate the tests so they run on every push. This creates a 'green-light' status that you can show potential clients as proof of your development standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that executes all tests on every push to the main branch and notifies the team of failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a .github/workflows/test.yml file that runs full test suite on push.'

steps.title: Generate 'Proof of Quality' Dashboard
steps.description: Create a public-facing or client-ready report of your test coverage. This is your new 'portfolio'—a document showing 95%+ coverage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary report of the current test suite coverage that can be exported as a professional PDF for client proposals.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a test coverage summary report and save it to /docs/quality-report.pdf'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Happy Path' testing before edge cases to ensure the business model actually functions.
Use test coverage as a sales metric; clients trust developers who show them their coverage reports.
Never ship code without a corresponding test; it is better to have a smaller feature set that works perfectly.
Use Claude Code to refactor your tests every two weeks to keep them clean and fast.
Document your testing philosophy in your README—it signals to enterprise clients that you are a serious operator.
content.next_steps: Integrate these tests into your production environment, push the badge to your landing page, and start pitching your 'Zero-Downtime' development methodology to prospects.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for scale. Your portfolio is not what you did yesterday; it is the quality of what you deliver today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
